**Generation Strategy:**
1. Distribution enforced exactly: 3 tech_literacy (easy MCQ + medium MCQ + hard open_ended), 3 reliability (easy MCQ + medium SJT + hard open_ended), 2 adaptability (medium SJT + hard MCQ), 1 communication (medium SJT), 1 leadership (hard SJT) — 5 MCQ + 3 SJT + 2 open_ended, 3 easy + 4 medium + 3 hard.
2. All scenarios grounded in Azerbaijan fintech/government context (banking app in Baku, Nakhchivan edge case, government e-service portal, Central Bank compliance) — not generic Western SaaS examples that would feel distant to the target user.
3. IRT parameters calibrated to difficulty tier: easy items use irt_b in [-1.8, -1.5] with moderate discrimination (1.1-1.2), medium items use irt_b in [0.2, 0.5] with higher discrimination (1.5-1.8), hard items use irt_b in [1.5, 2.0] with maximum discrimination (2.0-2.3) and low guessing parameters (0.08-0.12) — reflecting that hard QA questions require genuine expertise and are not guessable.
